assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The conventions.md file explicitly references analyzing sibling code patterns from advisory/endpoints/get.rs (line 44: 'Path<Id> following the existing pattern in advisory/endpoints/get.rs'), advisory/service/advisory.rs (conventions.md line 14-15: service methods verb_noun pattern, service structs AdvisoryService), advisory/model/summary.rs (plan.md line 43: 'AdvisorySummary — consistently at modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs in Implementation Notes'), and common/src/error.rs (conventions.md line 20: 'AppError enum is defined in common/src/error.rs'). At least 4 of the listed files are referenced.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan (plan.md lines 16-25) lists exactly 3 files to create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, advisory_summary.rs test file; and 3 files to modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs. The plan also explicitly notes in line 28 'Files NOT Modified: server/src/main.rs — No changes needed'. The 6 file description files (file-1 through file-6) correspond exactly to these 6 files. No unrelated files are modified.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 17-19 list: 1) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, 2) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, 3) tests/api/advisory_summary.rs. file-1-description.md (Action: CREATE for model), file-2-description.md (Action: CREATE for endpoint handler), file-3-description.md (Action: CREATE for test file) confirm all three.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 23-25 list: 1) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs, 2) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, 3) modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs. file-5-description.md (Action: MODIFY for service), file-6-description.md (Action: MODIFY for endpoints/mod.rs), file-4-description.md (Action: MODIFY for model/mod.rs) confirm all three.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 67-75 show the commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' which follows the type(scope): description format. The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201' which references the Jira ticket.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: The commit message section in plan.md (lines 66-75) does not mention --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' or any trailer. The commit message block contains only the conventional commit message with 'Implements TC-9201' footer but no Assisted-by trailer.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 13: 'Branch Name: TC-9201' explicitly names the branch. plan.md lines 9-10: 'Target Branch: main' references the target branch value.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md line 6-9 identifies the model/service/endpoints module structure: 'Each domain module follows the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ triple structure (observed in sbom/, advisory/, package/)'. conventions.md lines 19-20 identifies error handling: 'All handlers return Result<T, AppError> with .context() wrapping' and 'AppError enum is defined in common/src/error.rs and implements IntoResponse'. Both patterns are clearly identified.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 9-10: '## Target Branch\nmain' explicitly identifies 'main' as the target branch.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: No mention of 'description digest', 'digest comment', 'Step 1.5' related to digest checking, or 'backward compatibility' appears anywhere in plan.md, conventions.md, or any of the file description files. The plan does not address the description digest protocol at all.
